methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $11M to date. Valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable set of 9 private and 1 public company operating in the same sector, including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $11M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector companies at Series A stage—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yielding a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M to $200M. While the peer set is primarily at an advanced stage relative to Furno, it provides an anchor for the climate-tech sector.

Triangulating both methods produces a low bound of $23M, a median of $89M, and a high bound of $173M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around stage adjustment and the degree to which seed-stage execution risk should discount relative to series-stage comps. Valuation credibility depends critically on Furno's product development progress, capital efficiency, and clarity on go-to-market strategy relative to the peer cohort.
